title: dict split-keys: readers of a type's shared dk_nentries use plain loads instead of LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES (object.__getstate__, dict.clear) racing setattr's atomic bump
description: object.__getstate__ (object_getstate_default -> _PyObject_IsInstanceDictEmpty) iterates the type's shared-keys with a plain loop bound `i < keys->dk_nentries`; a concurrent setattr adding a NEW attribute name inserts a key and bumps dk_nentries via split_keys_entry_added's _Py_atomic_store_ssize_relaxed. The write was deliberately made atomic (comment: racing with reads) but this reader omitted the matching LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES atomic load. Pickling/copying an object while another thread does setattr is a data race on the type's shared-keys dk_nentries.
sites: Objects/dictobject.c insert_split_key:1940 (calls split_keys_entry_added after inserting a new shared key)
Objects/dictobject.c store_instance_attr_lock_held:7396 (setattr on inline-values instance -> insert_split_key)
Objects/dictobject.c _PyObject_IsInstanceDictEmpty:7666 (READ, plain: for i < keys->dk_nentries loop bound; should use LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES)
Objects/typeobject.c object___getstate___impl:8079 / object_getstate:8054 (object.__getstate__, also reached via pickle reduce_newobj / copy)
race_kind: shared split-keys dk_nentries: non-atomic reader vs atomic writer (multiple reader call sites)
shared_state: PyDictKeysObject.dk_nentries of a type's shared keys (CACHED_KEYS(tp)); grows when setattr first-touches a new attribute name, read as the inline-values iteration bound
severity: low (value-benign, crash-free; single aligned word, monotonically increasing; per-slot value read already atomic and array is capacity-sized, so no OOB/UAF). Real TSan data race on read-only-looking pickle/copy/__getstate__.
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66) within a few seconds; SUMMARY may name either side of the same read/write pair
suggested_fix: In _PyObject_IsInstanceDictEmpty (dictobject.c:7666) read the loop bound via LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES(keys) (= _Py_atomic_load_ssize_relaxed(&keys->dk_nentries)) instead of the plain keys->dk_nentries; relaxed ordering suffices. Non-free-threaded build unaffected (macro is a plain read there).
confirmed_unfixed_on: heads/main:bcf98ddbc40 (current main tip of the build, Jul 4 2026)
notes: Writer split_keys_entry_added:248 was deliberately made atomic ('increase before we decrease ... when we're racing with reads'); the reader in _PyObject_IsInstanceDictEmpty forgot the matching atomic load. The same file already uses the correct LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES macro (dict_equal, :4632). Audit other plain iterations of shared-keys dk_nentries. In scope: __getstate__+setattr is a normal method race, distinct from concurrent __init__/construction (cf. cpython#127192).

related_issues.note: Audit all built-in modules for thread safety -- the dict/split-keys hardening remit; no PR covers these specific reader sites

assessment: Real, NEW (no existing filing). The writer split_keys_entry_added bumps dk_nentries atomically ('when we're racing with reads'); these readers forgot the matching LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES (the atomic macro already exists at dictobject.c:237 and is used correctly at :4632). Fix: LOAD_KEYS_NENTRIES at the reader sites. Same shared-split-keys FT-hardening remit as gh-116738. Cross-check gh-116738/gh-115999 before filing.
reader_faces: clear_lock_held (dictobject.c:3110/3115, via dict.clear on a sibling instance)
